methods.description: This method calculates climate matching (l) and climate filtering (d) metrics using a null model to compare how taxa match their climate relative to expectations from sampling a regional pool.

steps.description: Calculate l and d metrics using a null model.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate climate matching and filtering metrics using null model

steps.description: Define D as the median distance between assemblage-inferred climate and random samples of niche values from taxa present in the assemblage.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate median distance for assemblage-inferred climate

steps.description: Define L as the vector between assemblage-inferred climate and observed climate.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate vector between inferred and observed climate

steps.description: Generate a null distribution of D and L by sampling assemblages of equal richness from the regional pool.
steps.searchable_summary: generate null distribution for D and L metrics

steps.description: Obtain standardized climate deviations (d and l) by comparing observed values of D and L to those generated under the regional expectation via z-transformation.

methods.description: This method defines two regional pools (basic and rare) to test the influence of common taxa on assembly signals and climate matching.

steps.description: Define basic and rare regional pools to test the influence of common taxa.
steps.searchable_summary: define basic and rare regional pools

steps.description: Compare assemblages from basic and rare pools for climate matching over the past 21,000 years.
steps.searchable_summary: compare climate matching in basic and rare pools

steps.description: Analyze the influence of broad-leaved taxa on climate filtering and permissiveness.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze broad-leaved taxa influence on climate filtering

methods.description: This method involves obtaining and processing pollen assemblage composition data and climate data for analysis.

steps.description: Obtain pollen assemblage composition data from Maguire et al. (2016).
steps.searchable_summary: fetch pollen assemblage data from Maguire et al. (2016)

steps.description: Process pollen data to identify count data to at least the genus level.
steps.searchable_summary: process pollen data to genus level

steps.description: Calculate the relative abundance of each genus/taxon relative to the total pollen sum.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate relative abundance of pollen taxa

steps.description: Linearly interpolate relative abundance data to estimate abundance at 500-year intervals.
Apply a taxon-and site-specific variable threshold corresponding to 5% of the maximum abundance recorded for each pollen type.
steps.searchable_summary: apply variable threshold to pollen abundance data
